sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Espanyol have shown decent form in their last 5 matches with 3 wins, 1 draw, and 1 loss, scoring consistently. Atlético Madrid have had mixed results with 3 wins and 2 losses, but they’ve demonstrated strong attacking potential in recent games.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two encounters between these teams, both matches ended in draws (1-1 and 0-0), indicating tight contests. Historically, Atlético have a slight edge, but Espanyol have been competitive at home.

sections.subtitle: Defensive and Offensive Stats
sections.text: Espanyol have managed to keep games close, often scoring first as seen in 6 of their last 7 matches. Atlético Madrid, despite conceding in some recent games, have a potent attack, averaging more shots per game than Espanyol.

sections.subtitle: Key Trends
sections.text: Both teams tend to see fewer than 10.5 corners per game in recent matches, and their head-to-heads often result in both teams scoring. Additionally, card counts have been high in past meetings, aligning with the referee’s average of 5.5 yellows per game.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Atlético Madrid leads Espanyol by a score of 1-0 in this La Liga encounter at Livens Jean Louis in Cornella. The solitary goal came from Julián Álvarez in the 37th minute, capitalizing on Atlético's dominant performance in the first half. Atlético Madrid controlled much of the game, holding a significant edge in possession and creating more dangerous opportunities in the final third. Espanyol, on the other hand, struggled to convert their limited chances and will need to regroup for a stronger showing in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance comparison
halftimeSections.text: Atlético Madrid showcased their superiority in the first half with a higher number of entries into the final third and penalty area compared to Espanyol. Their attacking intent was evident with more shots on target and corner kicks, which translated into a well-deserved lead. Espanyol, despite showing some resilience defensively, lacked the cutting edge in attack, managing fewer shots on target and struggling to maintain possession. Defensively, Espanyol held their ground in aerial duels, but their overall duel-winning rate matched Atlético's, indicating a balanced physical battle.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The momentum in the first half largely favored Atlético Madrid, especially in the latter stages where they consistently pushed forward with sustained attacking spells. Early in the game, Espanyol showed brief moments of promise with a few attacking surges, but these quickly faded as Atlético took control. The away side's persistent pressure around the goal-scoring minute was a clear indicator of their dominance, while Espanyol struggled to regain momentum and build cohesive attacks as the half progressed.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Atlético Madrid, Julián Álvarez has been the standout performer, not only scoring the opener but also posing a constant threat with his positioning and finishing. His influence in the final third will likely be crucial in the second half. On the Espanyol side, players like Leandro Cabrera and Roberto Fernández have shown glimpses of potential, with the former solid at the back and the latter looking to create opportunities. Espanyol will need more from their key attacking figures to turn the tide.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Atlético Madrid appears poised to maintain their control, especially if they continue to exploit spaces in Espanyol's defense. Their attacking depth and ability to dominate possession suggest they could add to their tally. Espanyol, however, will likely push for an equalizer, potentially taking more risks and opening up the game. If they can capitalize on any defensive lapses from Atlético, a comeback isn't entirely out of reach, but they’ll need to sharpen their finishing and build sustained pressure.
